note: Per-train facts for every named train in the Rail Atlas (data/atlas/train/itinerary.json — trips without a named train are rail-centered packages and have no entry here). `train` matches the itinerary spelling exactly for joins. Figures compiled July 2026 from operator materials — capacities and cabin counts shift with carriage refits; verify before quoting.

trains.cabins: Historic cabins, suites and 8 Grand Suites in restored 1920s–30s carriages
trains.notes: The definitive luxury sleeper; Grand Suites add en-suite baths and free-flowing champagne.

trains.route: Rome-based loops: Tuscany, Sicily, Venice, the south
trains.cabins: 31 cabins: 18 suites, 12 deluxe, 1 La Dolce Vita Suite
Twin, double and suite staterooms incl. two Grand Suites with Dior Spa carriages
trains.notes: Country-house party on rails; kilted piper, whisky ambassador.

trains.route: London-based circuits: Cornwall, Lake District, Wales
trains.notes: Belmond's first England/Wales sleeper, launched 2025; Wellness carriage aboard.

trains.cabins: Pullman, State and Presidential suites in teak-panelled carriages
trains.notes: The only true luxury sleeper in Southeast Asia; jungle-window dining.

trains.cabins: Deluxe double, twin and suite cabins with oxygen-enriched option
trains.notes: The highest luxury sleeper on earth (crosses 4,800m); pairs with the Hiram Bingham day train to Machu Picchu.

trains.route: Vancouver–Banff / Jasper (+ Rockies to the Red Rocks, US)
trains.cabins: No sleepers — GoldLeaf bi-level glass-dome coaches, hotel nights en route
trains.notes: Daylight-only by design so no scenery is slept through; GoldLeaf is the tier worth taking.

trains.cabins: Prestige class: 6–10 private cabins per departure with concierge
trains.notes: The value icon of transcontinental rail; Prestige class is the luxury play.

trains.route: Pretoria–Cape Town, Victoria Falls, Namibia, Dar es Salaam
trains.cabins: Pullman, Deluxe and Royal suites (Royal: full private bathroom with tub)
trains.notes: Rebuilt classic carriages; the Dar es Salaam run is Africa's great rail epic.
Sold by ballot months ahead; arguably the most exclusive train on earth.

trains.cabins: Day train — first-class compartments, no sleepers
trains.notes: The Glenfinnan Viaduct 'Harry Potter' run; appears inside longer Scotland itineraries.
